arts.ttl: Newcastle United squad revealed for Brentford with Howe weighing up rotation dilemma
arts.sum: Of course the break meant long journeys and appearances for their countries for several of the United squad, with the likes of Anthony Gordon, Brumo Guimaraes, Alexander Isak, Tino Livramento, Lewis Hall and Miguel Almiron all involved last month. After two weeks of 'inactivity' during the last international break, Newcastle United play their fourth game in less than fortnight today when they take on Brentford at the Gtech Community Stadium.

arts.ttl: Is Brentford vs Newcastle United on TV, what time is kick-off and how can I follow it?
arts.sum: After the drama of the midweek draw with Liverpool, Newcastle United face perhaps just as big a test today when they take on Brentford at the Gtech Community Stadium. No one has picked up more points on their own turf than Brentford this season, with six wins and a draw from their seven games at home this term.

arts.ttl: Newcastle United transfer decision means £40,000-a-week defender set for possible return
arts.sum: Newcastle United could be set for an unexpected reunion with left-back Jamal Lewis with Sao Paulo ready to end his loan deal early. We have a clause in his contract – Newcastle could call him up at the start of the season, and we could return him.

arts.ttl: I knew Unai Emery turned down Newcastle United job before I agreed terms with Amanda Staveley
arts.sum: Newcastle United boss Eddie Howe has admitted that he knew Amanda Staveley offered Unai Emery the job before him at St James' Park. Featuring on William Hill ’s Up Front with Simon Jordan, Howe said: "It doesn’t bother me that Unai Emery turned the Newcastle job down, not in the slightest.

arts.ttl: Jorge Mendes contacts Wolves and Newcastle United to sell player – €20-25m January deal
arts.sum: AreaNapoli journalist Gianluca Vitale has an interesting exclusive on Friday afternoon, regarding a Lens defender. Abdukodir Khusanov arrived at the French club in 2023 from FC Energetik-BGU Minsk. Unsurprisingly, that was a relatively cheap transfer, with the central defender costing only €100k. He’s gone on to shine for the Ligue 1 side, and this season The post Jorge Mendes contacts Wolves and Newcastle United to sell player – €20-25m January deal appeared first on Sport Witness.

arts.ttl: Chelsea Transfer News Today: Mudryk interest CONFIRMED, Isak TRUTH, Disasi to LEAVE?
arts.sum: The 23-year-old has been linked with a January exit, amid reported interest from the likes of Crystal Palace and Newcastle United, and Vadim Shabliy, his agent, has confirmed that clubs are monitoring his client. FootballTransfers runs through all the latest Chelsea transfer news, rumours and gossip, including the latest on Mykhailo Mudryk, Alexander Isak and Axel Disasi .

arts.ttl: Brentford vs Newcastle Preview: Team News, Predicted Lineups and Score Prediction
arts.sum: Brentford will look to maximise home advantage when they take on Newcastle in the Premier League on Saturday afternoon. Ahead of the clash, we’ve made a prediction for the game, provided our...

arts.ttl: Eddie Howe sees 'great signs' for popular £132m Newcastle United plan to continue into the future
arts.sum: The trio of choice in Eddie Howe's midfield has been anyone's guess at various stages of the season and that was no different on Wednesday as Joe Willock dropped out of the team to allow Bruno Guimaraes, Joelinton and Sandro Tonali to play alongside one another once again. “I understand the debate about the midfield,” Howe said after the St James' Park draw.

arts.ttl: Brentford vs Newcastle United predictions as 'brilliant' Magpies set for goalfest
arts.sum: Can Newcastle United continue to intensity they showed against Liverpool into this weekend's big game with Brentford? Brentford will score at home and I also expect Newcastle to have a go at this game.

arts.ttl: Another Newcastle v Liverpool classic and Chelsea up to second – Football Weekly Extra
arts.athr: Presented by Max Rushden, with Barry Glendenning, Troy Townsend, and Jordan Jarrett-Bryan. Produced by Silas Gray and Max Rushden. Executive produced by Phil Maynard.
arts.sum: Max Rushden is joined by Barry Glendenning, Troy Townsend and Jordan Jarrett-Bryan to discuss all the midweek Premier League games Rate, review, share on Apple Podcasts, Soundcloud, Audioboom, Mixcloud, Acast and Stitcher, and join the conversation on Facebook, Twitter and email. On the podcast today, the panel covers all of the midweek Premier League action, including a thrilling game at St James’ Park as Newcastle and Liverpool deliver one of the season's best games thus far. Fabien Schär’s last-minute equaliser salvaged a point after a Mo Salah brace nearly sealed it for the Reds. What does this mean for Liverpool in the title race? Continue reading...
